name: thinking-deeply
description: Engages structured analysis to explore multiple perspectives and context dependencies before responding. Use when users ask confirmation-seeking questions, make leading statements, request binary choices, or when feeling inclined to quickly agree or disagree without thorough consideration.
capabilities: Detects confirmation-seeking questions and leading statements
Identifies embedded assumptions in user queries
Provides context-dependent recommendations with clear reasoning
Transforms binary choices into balanced trade-off analyses
Delivers responses using structured analysis framework
author: brunoasm
securitySummary: All 45 static findings are false positives. This skill contains only Markdown documentation files (SKILL.md, README.md) with prompt instructions. No executable code, scripts, network operations, or system access exists. Static scanner incorrectly flagged Markdown code formatting as Ruby backtick execution, documentation URLs as malicious network calls, and analytical thinking instructions as system reconnaissance.
valueStatement: AI assistants often provide quick answers without exploring nuances. This skill enforces structured multi-perspective analysis before responding, ensuring answers consider context, trade-offs, and alternative viewpoints.
limitations: Does not access external data or APIs
Only activates on trigger patterns, not continuous analysis
Does not modify system files or execute code
Cannot verify factual claims against external sources

useCases.title: Evaluate Technology Choices
useCases.description: Get balanced framework comparisons instead of biased recommendations for programming languages, libraries, and architectural patterns.

useCases.title: Make Informed Decisions
useCases.description: Receive context-dependent analysis of trade-offs before committing to product strategies, feature prioritization, or technical approaches.

useCases.title: Explore Multiple Viewpoints
useCases.description: Get comprehensive analysis that considers alternative perspectives and hidden factors in complex analytical questions.

promptTemplates.title: Framework Comparison
promptTemplates.prompt: Is React better than Vue for my project?

promptTemplates.title: Architectural Decision
promptTemplates.prompt: Should we use microservices for our new application?

promptTemplates.title: Tool Selection
promptTemplates.prompt: Should I use TypeScript or JavaScript for this project?

promptTemplates.title: Complex Trade-offs
promptTemplates.prompt: Should we build or buy this software component?

bestPractices: Use when asking about technology choices, architectural decisions, or strategic recommendations
Apply the reframing step to identify the real underlying question behind the user's prompt
Always identify context dependencies that influence recommendations
antiPatterns: Do not accept the user's framing without questioning embedded assumptions
Avoid giving simple yes or no answers without exploring trade-offs
Do not present only one perspective as universally correct

faq.question: When does this skill activate?
faq.answer: It triggers on confirmation questions, leading statements, binary choices, and situations where you feel inclined to quickly agree or disagree.

faq.question: Does this skill access external APIs?
faq.answer: No. This is a pure prompt template that modifies response behavior without external network calls.

faq.question: Can I customize when it triggers?
faq.answer: Yes. Modify the description field in Skill.md to make triggering more specific or broader.

faq.question: Will it slow down responses?
faq.answer: Minimal overhead. The structured analysis adds brief processing time for better quality responses.

faq.question: What questions benefit most from this skill?
faq.answer: Technology comparisons, architectural decisions, tool selection, and any choice with significant trade-offs.

faq.question: Is this skill safe for production use?
faq.answer: Yes. It contains no executable code, only prompt instructions that guide reasoning process.
